emails.subject: eThink About It: July 31, 2000
emails.body: Vince This correspondence is sent to you in response to the below question=20 pertaining to the type of research that should be introduced to Enron On Tuesday April 4th, 2000, a local newspaper in my area "The Bakersfie= ld=20 California" ran an article pertaining to the 50 million dollars of funding= =20 from the U.S. congress set aside to study deep sea floor extraction of=20 methane hydrate. This study will focus on methane hydrates as an energy=20 source and the technologies needed for safe, efficient development. =20 According to this article, the ice-like deposits of frozen methane have an= =20 energy potential equal to more than twice that of all other fossil fuels=20 combined. I can fax you a copy of this newspaper article if you would like to read it= .=20 Send me you FAX number or call me at Enron Wind Corp. 661-823-6433.=20 Also when I was in engineering student back in 1985, I did an independent= =20 senior level study on a proposed method which possibly could be used to=20 extract hydrate from the sea floor. If you are interested, I could e-mail y= ou=20 a copy of my thesis explaining this proposed method .=20 Carl Colditz Test Engineer Enron Wind Corp. 13681 Chantico Road Tehachapi, CA 93561 661-823-6433 661-823-6804 (fax) carl.colditz@enron.com Forwarded by Carl Colditz/EWC/Enron on 07/31/2000=20 11:48 AM The eThink Team From: eThink on 07/28/2000 05:58 PM CDT Sent by: Enron Announcements

emails.subject: Re: eThink About It: July 31, 2000
emails.body: Re: eThink About It: July 31, 2000 Carl, Thanks for the message. My fax is 713 646 2503. Vince Carl Colditz@ENRON 07/31/2000 03:32 PM
